channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government to ban celebrities and athletes from gambling ads
item.description: The Australian government has announced a significant change to gambling advertising rules, set to affect the nation's sports and entertainment industries. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese revealed tha...

item.title: Sindh Government to Demolish and Reconstruct Fire-Damaged Gul Plaza
item.description: KARACHI: Sindh Chief Minister Syed Murad Ali Shah has issued directives to demolish the fire-damaged Gul Plaza building to begin reconstruction after a tragic fire on January 17 claimed dozens of live...

item.title: Promising Teen Athletes Making Their Mark Across Sports
item.description: A range of teenage athletes have already begun to leave their mark on the global sports scene, spanning disciplines from Formula 1 and skateboarding to darts. These young talents are showcasing impres...

item.title: Elsa Majimbo Clarifies Rumours, Shares Her New Lifestyle Choices
item.description: US-based Kenyan TikTok star Elsa Majimbo has addressed online rumours claiming she uses drugs, following changes in her lifestyle. The comedian, who gained attention after posting a controversial phot...

item.title: Government Steps Up Oversight on GLP-1 Drugs Amid Rising Generic Sales
item.description: The Indian government has increased its monitoring and regulatory measures over GLP-1 drugs, as generic versions flood the market and prices drop significantly. These medications, primarily used for t...

item.title: Top Nigerian Newspaper Headlines for Thursday, 2nd April 2026
item.description: Good morning Naija! Today, President Bola Tinubu don cancel him trip to Ogun State wey suppose make him dey for Iperu for the launch of Gateway International Cargo Airport, and e don decide to travel ...

item.title: Combat-Related Fatalities Drop Significantly in March Amid Military Operations
item.description: In March 2026, Pakistan saw a notable reduction in combat-related fatalities, recording a 35 percent decrease compared to February, according to the Pakistan Institute for Conflict and Security Studie...

item.title: Nyeri Family Searches for Missing PP1 Pupil Who Vanished While Playing Outside Home
item.description: A family in Nyeri County is in deep distress as their young son, Travis Wanjohi, a PP1 pupil at Blue Rose Academy, went missing while playing outside their home at the Veterinary Staff Residential Qua...

item.title: Iran denies requesting pause on US energy strikes amid escalating Middle East conflict
item.description: Tensions between Iran, Israel, and the United States have sharply escalated, with strikes targeting Iranian industrial and military infrastructure. Israel reportedly attacked two of Iran's largest ste...

item.title: Manila Archbishop Discusses Mental Health Support for Priests at Chrism Mass
item.description: During the annual Chrism Mass, Manila Archbishop Jose Cardinal Advincula highlighted the importance of mental well-being among clergy. Emphasizing the concept 'name it to tame it,' the archbishop enco...

item.title: Gauteng Cabinet Reshuffle Sees Dunga Take Over Finance, Chiloane Moves to Arts and Education
item.description: Gauteng Premier Panyaza Lesufi has undertaken a cabinet reshuffle affecting key provincial portfolios. The reshuffle sees Nkululeko Dunga of the Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) appointed as the new Fi...

item.title: Ogun State spends billions on official vehicles while road network remains in poor condition
item.description: For Ogun State, motorists dey struggle daily with potholes, broken roads, and dangerous stretches wey don claim lives for Adiyan-Orudu, Ilaro-Ibese, and many other areas. Investigations show say more ...

item.title: Reflecting on Easter: Faith, Liberation, and Hope in South Africa
item.description: In this personal reflection, Malusi Mpumlwana explores the profound significance of Easter within the context of South Africa's struggle against apartheid. He traces the origins of Easter as a celebra...

item.title: Regina Pats fall behind in playoff series after Game 4 defeat
item.description: The Regina Pats are now on the brink of elimination from their playoff series following a 4-2 defeat against the Medicine Hat Tigers in Game 4. Despite competitive play throughout the match, the Pats ...

item.title: Positive Leadership Approaches for Transforming Kenya
item.description: The article argues that Kenya’s leaders can drive meaningful change without resorting to personal attacks or costly rallies. Auratic Koome emphasises the power of simple initiatives, like the youth vo...

item.title: Joburg Giants fall to third consecutive defeat in BAL against RSSB Tigers
item.description: The Joburg Giants suffered their third straight loss in the Basketball Africa League after a 102-89 defeat to Rwanda's RSSB Tigers at the SunBet Arena in Pretoria. Despite a spirited performance, led ...

item.title: Governance reforms key to restoring Nairobi's infrastructure
item.description: Nairobi is facing serious urban challenges, with traffic jams, water shortages, polluted rivers, and overflowing waste sites highlighting weaknesses in governance and infrastructure. A new Sh80 billio...

item.title: Trump claims progress in Iran conflict while PM announces $1b support for Australian businesses

item.title: UK to Convene International Talks on Reopening Strategic Hormuz Strait
item.description: The United Kingdom is set to host a high-level virtual meeting involving about 35 countries to discuss reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz, which has been effectively closed due to the ongoing Mi...

item.title: Nate Richert, former Sabrina the Teenage Witch actor, reunites with co-stars after two decades away from acting
item.description: Nate Richert, best known for playing Harvey Kinkle on the 1990s television series Sabrina the Teenage Witch, has reappeared in the public eye following a long hiatus from acting. Richert, now 47, had ...
